channel.title: Projects on rigle.dev
channel.description: Recent content in Projects on rigle.dev

item.title: 3dslibris
item.description:
Homebrew ebook and manga reader for Nintendo 3DS, based on the original Nintendo DS project dslibris.

Features

  • Library browser with grid and list views, cover thumbnails, metadata titles, and touch navigation.
  • Six reading themes with matching splash screens and reader gradients.
  • Formats: EPUB, FB2, TXT, RTF, ODT, MOBI, PDF, CBZ and XPS.
  • EPUB reflow with TOC, bookmarks, go-to-page, cached pagination, and broad inline/block formatting support.
  • Fixed-layout viewer for manga and documents (CBZ, PDF, XPS) with zoom, pan, outline navigation, and full-page preview.
  • Bundled fallback fonts for broader language coverage (Latin, Cyrillic, Greek, CJK, Arabic, Hebrew, Thai, and more).

Installation

Recommended — .cia via Universal-Updater:

item.title: El Último Frame
item.description:
About the podcast

El Último Frame was a podcast focused on the competitive Super Smash Bros. community in Spanish, featuring in-depth interviews with players, tournament organizers, content creators, and other key figures in the scene.

Platforms:

  • YouTube
  • Spotify
  • Apple Podcasts

Episode list

  • #16 - SmashBrosSpain, Smash Fighters and streaming | YouTube Over 10 years in the community, team growth and mentoring experience. With Bardo.
  • #15 - Working with Nintendo, Speedruns and Twitch inclusion | YouTube Nintendo collaboration, speedrunning and streaming in the community. With MTSergi.
  • #14 - How to cast, working with Riot Games and editing for BarbeQ | YouTube Professional development as a caster, work with Riot Games and video editing projects. With Aitor Tribal.
  • #13 - Travels, Smash Argentina and Wygers sponsorship | YouTube Competitive evolution, the Smash scene in South America and sponsorship details. With ZanT.
  • #12 Part 2 - Smash training, sponsorship and streaming | YouTube Training methodologies, getting sponsored and streaming plans. With Pepo.
  • #12 Part 1 - Personal life, evolution from Brawl and anecdotes | YouTube Personal history, progression from the Brawl era and competitive experiences. With Pepo.
  • #11 - Tronchabúfalos, Vidad’s father | YouTube Unique perspective on family support and tournament inclusivity improvements. With Tronchabúfalos.
  • #10 - ZaWarudo, Smash YouTuber | YouTube Aragon’s regional scene and YouTube channel growth. With ZaWarudo.
  • Five Questions #3 | YouTube Quick five-question format with a tournament organizer and streamer. With Charlie.
  • Five Questions #2 | YouTube Five questions with a top-16 ranking player. With Garrote.
  • Five Questions #1 | YouTube Introductory episode of the five-question format. With AlexGir.
  • #9 - Yagg, creator of the Starman League | YouTube Tournament organization and competitive positioning. With Yagg.

#8 - Roymi, organizer of Gaming Sisters | YouTube Competitive events focused on female players. With Roymi.

item.title: ROMHacking and translations
item.description:
How it started

When I got into programming and started wondering what was inside video games, I tried to “open them up” to see how they were made. I started by modifying Pokémon games to add more trainers, customize my experience or catch all Pokémon in a single cartridge. Yes, I began this journey doing “pokehacking”.

The biggest project I achieved in this field was the complete localization of the Nintendo DS game The World Ends With You. We opened the game, translated the text and images, and even dubbed the videos into Spanish. If you want to know more, I wrote a Twitter thread explaining it.
